About the Bookstore

A demonstration application for the Retold ecosystem.

What Is This?

The Bookstore application showcases how pict-section-recordset integrates with retold-harness Meadow endpoints to provide a complete CRUD management experience. It combines authentication via pict-section-login with route-based navigation and dynamic recordset views.

Architecture

This application is built on the Retold module ecosystem. The backend is powered by retold-harness, which auto-generates RESTful CRUD endpoints from Meadow schema definitions. The harness serves a bookstore schema with entities for Books, Authors, BookStores, Reviews, Pricing, and Inventory.

On the frontend, Pict provides the MVC framework. Views are registered with the application and rendered into designated DOM containers. The pict-section-recordset metacontroller manages configuration-driven record list, read, create, and dashboard views with auto-generated filtering from the Meadow schema.

The Retold Ecosystem

Retold is a suite of 60+ JavaScript/Node.js modules for building web applications and APIs. The ecosystem follows a service provider pattern where modules register with a Fable instance and gain access to logging, configuration, and other services through dependency injection.

Key module groups include Fable (core DI and configuration), Meadow (data access and ORM), Orator (API server), Pict (MVC views and templates), and Utility (build tools and manifests).
